readings.summary: Jesus is taken up into heaven.

readings.citation:
Ps 47:2-3, 6-7, 8-9
God mounts his throne to shouts of joy:
a blare of trumpets for the Lord.
readings.antiphon:
Ps 47:2-3, 6-7, 8-9
God mounts his throne to shouts of joy:
a blare of trumpets for the Lord.

readings.summary: Christ has led the way for us to enter the sanctuary.

Go and teach all nations, says the Lord;
I am with you always, until the end of the world.
Jesus commissions the eleven, ascends to heaven.

antiphons.citation: Acts 1:11 — At the Mass during the Day
antiphons.text:
Men of Galilee, why gaze in wonder at the heavens?
This Jesus whom you saw ascending into heaven
will return as you saw him go, alleluia.

Christ, offering a single sacrifice for sins,
is seated for ever at God’s right hand, alleluia.

antiphons.citation: Communion Antiphon | Mt 28:20 (At Mass during the Day)
antiphons.text:
Behold, I am with you always,
even to the end of the age, alleluia.
